mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a win rate below 45% over a 5-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5%, rising inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in >70% of months where CPI growth surpasses the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 48% across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion and higher aggregate corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day equity returns, with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The double-dip signal produces positive forward returns in >65% of historical occurrences.
